metadata.source: Buki di oro — Ortografia i Lista di palabra Papiamentu, FPI, Kòrsou 2009
metadata.description: Complete orthography rules for Curaçao Papiamentu, encoded from all 13 chapters

II_letter_usage.description: Chapter II — Uso di diferente lèter en partikular

restricted_consonants.rule: c, j, q, x ONLY used in: digraphs (ch, dj, zj), math/chemistry, proper names, foreign words
restricted_consonants.c: Only in ch digraph, math, chemistry, proper names (Cornelio), geographic names (Connecticut), foreign words (centerfielder)
restricted_consonants.j: Only in dj digraph, proper names (Jacobo), foreign words (joyeria)
restricted_consonants.q: Only in proper names (Aquiles), geographic (Quebec), foreign words (quo vadis)
restricted_consonants.x: Only in proper names (Xiomara), foreign words (x-ray)

II_letter_usage.double_vowel_rule: When two vowels are heard, write both: poseé, kreensia, reeligí, reeduká, kooperá, koordiná, oochi
II_letter_usage.diphthong_after_g_rule: After velar g, write ui/ue as wi/we: lingwista (not linguïsta), ungwento (not unguënto), antigwedat, bèrgwensa, pingwino
II_letter_usage.rising_diphthong_rule: No word or syllable starts with a rising diphthong (ia,ie,iè,io,ua,ue,uo). Use y/w instead: bayena (not baiena), yabi (not iabi), wòri (not uòri)
II_letter_usage.no_y_between_i_vowel: No consonant y between vowel i and another vowel: Antia (not Antiya), biudo (not biyudo), brièchi (not briyèchi). Exception: biyaya
II_letter_usage.no_w_between_u_vowel: No consonant w between vowel u and another vowel: nua, yuana, bruera, duo. Exceptions: guènguwe, púluwe

double_consonants.rule: Double consonants appear in compounds and -nan plurals

word_types.skèrpi: Polysyllabic words stressed on last syllable (e.g. sapaté, restorant, artifisial)
word_types.grave: Words stressed on penultimate syllable (e.g. baki, sòrsaka, artefakto)
word_types.esdrúhulo: Words stressed on antepenultimate syllable (e.g. íntimo, penúltimo, katedrátiko)
word_types.sobresdrúhulo: Words stressed before antepenultimate (e.g. álablanka, áwaseru, ántayera)

acute_accent_rules: All skèrpi words ending in a vowel get acute: piská, karné, sintí, kachó, kurú
All grave words ending in a consonant get acute: fásil, difísil, fértil, hóben, miéntras
All esdrúhulo words get acute: último, sílaba, mérito, página, úniko
All sobresdrúhulo words get acute: álablanka, áwaseru, ántayera
III_accentuation.suffix_accent_preservation: Words with -mente or -nan suffixes keep original accent: íntimamente, piskánan, chikínan, fásilmente, páginanan
exceptions: If tonic syllable already has grave accent (è,ò,ù), no acute added: sènguènè, Skèrpènè, èlebog
No acute on skèrpi words ending in descending diphthong or triphthong: kabai, Kòrsou, kukui, bakiou
No acute on skèrpi words ending in a consonant: paraf, barak, animal, komishon, pais
No acute on grave words ending in -el, -en, -er: baiskel, mangel, deken, koper, binager
No acute on grave words ending in a vowel: rabia, kunuku, feila
tabata and tabatin written without accent (exception to esdrúhulo rule)
Not necessary to put accent on capital letters

IV_syllable_division.rule: Divide by pronunciation. Each syllable has at least one vowel. Never split diphthongs, triphthongs, or digraphs (ch, dj, sh, zj)
IV_syllable_division.line_break_rule: Don't leave a single vowel alone at end of line: avoid a-la, a-breviá, è-zè

capitalize: First letter at start of text
First letter after . ? ! and sometimes after : or ...
Proper names of people, animals, objects
Names of deity and divine persons
Titles of dignity: Sumo Pontífise, Su Mahestat
do_NOT_capitalize: Days of the week: djadumingu, djaluna, djamars, djárason, djaweps, djabièrnè, djasabra
Months: yanüari, febrüari, mart, aprel, mei, yüni, yüli, ougùstùs, sèptèmber, òktober, novèmber, desèmber
Words derived from geographic names: chines, ingles, franses, hulandes, merikano

VII_numerals.large_numbers_rule: mil, mion, bion are written separately from other numerals: sinku mil seisshentidiesshete
VII_numerals.currency_example: fl. 2.326.719,58 = dos mion tresshentibintiseis mil sheteshentidiesnuebe florin ku sinkuentiocho sèn

rules: Geographic names capitalized: countries, areas, cities, mountains, rivers, deserts, celestial bodies
In non-scientific text: luna, solo, mundu (lowercase)
Derivatives of geographic names lowercase: chines, ingles, franses, hulandes, surafrikano
Phonological spelling allowed among PA speakers: Merka, Spaña, Niu Yòrk, Kòrsou, Ruba, Boneiru
For international correspondence use international spelling: Curaçao (not Kòrsou)

IX_contractions.general_rule: Avoid contractions when possible. Use apostrophe where letters are elided.
IX_contractions.pronoun_rule: Personal pronouns as object written separately: duna mi, duna bo, duna e / dun'é, duna nos, duna boso, duna nan

X_days_months.note: Days and months are NOT capitalized in Papiamentu

rules: If a foreign word has NOT adapted to Papiamentu pronunciation, write it in its original orthography: leuk, gewoon, safe, gouverneur, joya
All other words follow Papiamentu orthography rules: atverbio, buldòk, bulfait, bòikòt, winshil, brek

XII_terminology.description: Chapter XII — Aklarashon di algun término

key_terms.dígrafo: two letters for one sound: ch in chapi
key_terms.diptongo: combination of vowel with i or u
key_terms.triptongo: combination of diphthong with i or u: iau, ieu, iou, uai, uei
key_terms.homógrafo: same spelling different meaning: mata (v.) kill vs mata (sus.) plant

spelling_transforms.description: Key orthographic transforms for the correction engine

c_to_k.rule: Replace c with k in Papiamentu words (c only in ch digraph, math, proper names, foreign words)

j_to_dj_or_y.rule: Spanish j becomes y or dj: julio→yüli, jueves→djaweps
j_to_dj_or_y.note: j only used in dj digraph in native Papiamentu

common_spanish_transforms.v_to_b_patterns: Some v→b but v is kept in most words: verbo stays verbo
common_spanish_transforms.h_silent_to_h_sound: In Papiamentu h is always pronounced: hasi, hende, hòmber
